title: Small-Signal Log: Recover the Saturating-Resonant Response Law
strategies: constant baseline: predict the mean training response (ignores frequency and amplitude entirely)

quadratic-surface recipe: ordinary-least-squares y = c0+c1*f+c2*a+c3*f^2+c4*a^2+c5*f*a; tracks the mild curvature visible inside the small-signal/sub-resonant training box but has no saturation ceiling and no resonance peak, so it explodes or keeps climbing monotonically once extrapolated into the large-signal / near-and-past-resonance held-out region

structural recovery: exploit the stated SEPARABLE product structure -- for any candidate (f0, w, As), z(f,a)=tanh(a/As)/(1+((f-f0)/w)**2) makes the whole surface a single linear regressor y~p*z; grid-search only the three shape parameters, the gain drops out via 1-D least squares
invalid: emit a disallowed token (attribute access) -> Ratio 0.0
